title: lathyrus palustris var. myrtifolius
intro: Lathyrus palustris var. myrtifolius, also known as Marsh Pea or Marsh Vetchling, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Lathyrus palustris var. myrtifolius include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water or oil, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
